content:
{
  "concerns": [
    {
      "id": "SKP-001",
      "concern": "Cross-runtime locking mechanism mismatch will cause cache corruption",
      "severity": "CRITICAL",
      "severity_score": 900,
      "why_matters": "The SDD specifies bash uses `flock`, Python uses `fcntl.flock`, and TS uses `proper-lockfile`. `proper-lockfile` uses directory/file creation for locking, NOT POSIX advisory locks (`fcntl`). The TS runtime will completely ignore Python/Bash locks, leading to race conditions and corrupted JSON cache files.",
      "location": "Section 4.2.3 flock contract",
      "recommendation": "Standardize on a single locking mechanism across all runtimes, or write a native TS wrapper that explicitly uses POSIX `fcntl` advisory locks to ensure cross-runtime mutual exclusion."
    },
    {
      "id": "SKP-002",
      "concern": "Bedrock integration vastly underestimates authentication complexity",
      "severity": "CRITICAL",
      "severity_score": 850,
      "why_matters": "Assuming Bedrock slots in as a 'peer provider' with just an `endpoint_family` ignores AWS SigV4 signing, region requirements, and boto3/credential-chain dependencies. A simple Bearer token (like OpenAI/Anthropic) will not work, breaking the unified adapter pattern.",
      "location": "Section 3.3 Per-model required fields & [ASSUMPTION-2]",
      "recommendation": "Explicitly define the AWS auth strategy (e.g., SigV4 interceptors for curl/TS, boto3 for Python) and add required schema fields for AWS Region and IAM role/profile."
    },
    {
      "id": "SKP-003",
      "concern": "Shadow pricing will artificially exhaust real API budgets",
      "severity": "HIGH",
      "severity_score": 750,
      "why_matters": "Deducting 'shadow' costs of subscription models from the real `cost_check_budget` will drain the budget limit. This will cause premature `BUDGET_EXHAUSTED` blockers for actual pay-per-token models, causing system-wide outages based on fake spend.",
      "location": "Section 3.3 & Section 9.4 Budget + abort policy",
      "recommendation": "Separate shadow pricing into a distinct tracking metric (`shadow_cost_micro_usd`) that does NOT decrement the hard enforcement budget used for pay-per-token providers."
    },
    {
      "id": "SKP-004",
      "concern": "Fail-open probe exacerbates local network outages",
      "severity": "HIGH",
      "severity_score": 700,
      "why_matters": "If the local network is down, the probe fails open and allows the actual LLM invocation to proceed. Instead of failing fast (2s), the system will hang for the full LLM timeout (potentially minutes) for every request, causing massive thread/process starvation.",
      "location": "Section 4.2.2 Public API (Probe semantics #5)",
      "recommendation": "If `detect_local_network()` returns False, the system should fast-fail the invocation with `LOCAL_NETWORK_FAILURE` rather than failing open and attempting doomed long-polling calls."
    },
    {
      "id": "SKP-005",
      "concern": "Thundering herd on probe cache expiry",
      "severity": "HIGH",
      "severity_score": 680,
      "why_matters": "When the 60s TTL expires, multiple concurrent requests will block on the lock. If the lock is held during the 2s network probe, all requests stall. If the lock is released before the network call, all requests will fire redundant probes, hitting rate limits.",
      "location": "Section 4.2.2 & 4.2.3",
      "recommendation": "Implement a stale-while-revalidate pattern. Serve the stale cache result immediately while one background thread/process acquires the lock to refresh the probe."
    },
    {
      "id": "SKP-006",
      "concern": "Weekly smoke fleet is too infrequent for operational alerting",
      "severity": "HIGH",
      "severity_score": 650,
      "why_matters": "LLM provider degradations happen in minutes/hours. A weekly cron job (Mondays at 13:00 UTC) is practically useless for 'active alerting' (HC6) and will only catch long-term deprecations, missing actual operational outages.",
      "location": "Section 9.1 Workflow",
      "recommendation": "Change the smoke fleet cron to run at least hourly, and adjust the budget/abort policy to accommodate the increased frequency."
    },
    {
      "id": "SKP-007",
      "concern": "Regex-based drift detection is highly brittle",
      "severity": "MEDIUM",
      "severity_score": 550,
      "why_matters": "The hardcoded regex `claude-[0-9]|gpt-[0-9]|gemini-[0-9]` will fail to catch new model naming conventions (e.g., `o1-preview`, `o3-mini`, `nova-pro`). This guarantees the CI gate will silently allow raw IDs for new models.",
      "location": "Section 5.4 Drift-CI scanner",
      "recommendation": "Instead of regex matching, extract the actual model IDs from `model-config.yaml` at CI runtime and dynamically build the search pattern, or enforce a strict wrapper function via AST parsing."
    },
    {
      "id": "SKP-008",
      "concern": "Runtime cycle detection aborts instead of skipping",
      "severity": "MEDIUM",
      "severity_score": 500,
      "why_matters": "If a cycle is detected during `walk_fallback_chain`, it raises `RoutingMissError` and aborts the entire chain. A misconfiguration in an alias shouldn't cause a hard outage if there are other valid fallback models available in the chain.",
      "location": "Section 5.3 Fallback walk algorithm",
      "recommendation": "Modify the walker to log a WARN, skip the duplicated/cyclic reference, and continue yielding the remaining unvisited models in the chain."
    },
    {
      "id": "SKP-009",
      "concern": "Unbounded audit log growth on disk",
      "severity": "MEDIUM",
      "severity_score": 450,
      "why_matters": "Emitting `model.invoke.complete` on every multi-model invocation with a 30-day retention policy (based on time, not size) risks filling up the `.run/audit/` disk space on high-throughput operator machines.",
      "location": "Section 6.2 Audit events emitted",
      "recommendation": "Add a maximum file size limit (e.g., 100MB) to the retention policy, triggering log rotation or archiving regardless of the 30-day time window."
    },
    {
      "id": "SKP-010",
      "concern": "Legacy quarantine creates a false sense of rollback safety",
      "severity": "LOW",
      "severity_score": 350,
      "why_matters": "Keeping the legacy bash adapter for 1 cycle as a 'kill switch' fallback is dangerous. The legacy adapter won't be updated for new schema changes or models during that cycle. If a rollback is actually needed, the legacy code will likely crash on the new config format.",
      "location": "Section 8.2 Quarantine vs delete",
      "recommendation": "Ensure the `tests/cycle-102/legacy-
